zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Adam de Cailly於1192年出生在英國諾福爾可時，他的父親John de Cailly27歲，母親Margaret29歲。他於大約1214年與Mabel Giffard在英國越可爾諾曼東結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。他去世於1262年享年70歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Cailly
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French:
topographic name for a person living on a patch of pebbly or stony soil, from Old French caillou ‘pebble, stone’, a word of Celtic origin. Compare Lacaille 2.

variants.name: Currier
meanings: English: occupational name for a person who dressed leather after it was tanned, from Middle English curr(e)iour (Old French conreeur ‘currier’).
Americanized form of French Caillé (see Caille 3) and Carrière (see Carriere ).

variants.name: Lacaille
meanings: French:
metonymic occupational name for someone who caught and sold quails, a variant of Caille 2, with fused feminine definite article la.

topographic name for a person living on a patch of pebbly or stony soil, a variant of Caille 1, with fused feminine definite article la; or a habitational name from La Caille, the name of several places in various parts of France. Compare Lacoy .

variants.name: Caye
meanings: French (mainly Lorraine): possibly a topographic name from Old French caie ‘sandbank, dike’.
French: nickname from dialect caye ‘sow’.
Possibly also an altered form of French   Caille   or   Caillet   (or of some other like-sounding surname); compare   Caya  .

variants.name: Cayer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Adelard, Armand, Normand, Jacques, Lucien, Andre, Benoit, Emile, Fernand, Germaine, Jean-Marc, Jean-Marie.
Altered form of French Caillé (see Caille 3). Compare Cayea .
French: from Old French caier, a term denoting a pack of four candles (from Latin quaterni), possibly applied as a metonymic occupational name for a candle merchant.

variants.name: Caya
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Adrien, Aurore, Cecile, Girard, Jacques, Normand.

Altered form of French Cailla, itself a dialect form of Caille 2 ‘quail’ (or perhaps an altered form of some other similar surname beginning in Caill-) or a habitational name from a minor place called Cailla. Compare Cayo .

variants.name: Caillet
meanings: French:

topographic name for a person living on a patch of pebbly or stony soil, or a habitational name from (Le) Caillet, the name of several places in various parts of France, from a diminutive of   Caille   1.
derivative of   Cail   3 ‘curds’, a metonymic occupational name for a dairy worker.

nameMeanings.name: Adam
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Gaelic equivalent of Adam .

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Adam de Cailly was born in 1192, in Norfolk, England, his father, John de Cailly, was 27 and his mother, Margaret, was 29. He married Mabel Giffard about 1214, in Normanton, Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. He died in 1262, at the age of 70.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Adam de Cailly est né en 1192, à Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, John de Cailly, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Margaret, avait 29 ans. Il a épousé Mabel Giffard environ 1214, à Normanton, Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Il est décédé en 1262, à l’âge de 70 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Adam de Caillyが1192年にイギリスノーフォルクで生まれた時，父John de Caillyは27歳，母Margaretは29歳であった。約1214年にイギリスヨークシュアイアノーマントンでMabel Giffardと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼は1262年に70歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Adam de Cailly(이)가 1192년 영국 노르폴크에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John de Cailly(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret(은)는 29세였다. 그는 대략 1214년 영국 요르크실르 노먼턴에서 Mabel Giffard(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1262년 70세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Adam de Cailly родился в 1192 г. в местности норфолк, Англия, его отцу по имени John de Cailly было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени Mabel Giffard около 1214 г. в местности нормантон, еркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Он умер в 1262 г. в возрасте 70 лет.
